# Google BigQuery Connector - Full Documentation

# Google BigQuery Connector 1.2 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following examples show several Mule flows for Google BigQuery Connector:

- [Get a Job](#get-job)
- [Insert a Job](#insert-job)
- [Delete a Job](#delete-job)
- [Get a Query Result](#get-query-result)
- [Cancel a Job](#cancel-job)
- [List Jobs](#list-jobs)
- [Query](#query)
- [Use On Table Row](#on-table-row)
- [Authorize](#authorize)
- [Render an HTML Form](#html-form)

## Configure a Connection

To secure connections, you must specify the OAuth connection field values. To do this:

- [Create a configuration file for a connection.](#create-config-file)
- [Configure the connection global elements.](#configure-conn)
- [Configure a global element for the properties file.](#configure-global)

### Create a Configuration File for a Connection

Create a configuration file that includes properties for a connection:

1.  Create a file named `mule-app.properties` in the `/src/main/resources/` folder.
2.  In the `mule-app.properties` file, create a set of properties for the connection, similar to the
    ones that follow, replacing the bracketed text (including the brackets) with the correct values
    for your configuration:

    config.consumerKey=<consumerkey> config.consumerSecret=<consumersecret>

    This may vary depending on the selected connection configuration.

For more information about creating a properties file, refer to
[Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure).

### Configure the Connection Global Elements

Configure global elements for connection:

1.  Create a new Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange** and enter `google bigquery`.
3.  Add **Google BigQuery Connector** to the **Selected modules** section and click **Finish**.
4.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
5.  Select **Connector Configuration > Google BigQuery Config** and click **OK**.
6.  Enter the values to configure either JWT or OAuth2. For more information about what values to
    enter in the fields for the connector configuration, see
    [Configure a Global Element for the Connector](google-bigquery-connector-studio#configure-global-element).
7.  Click the **Test Connection** button to ensure there is connectivity with the Google API. A
    successful message should pop up.
8.  Click **OK**.
9.  Open the HTTPS Listener config in **Global Element Configuration**.
10. Click the **TLS** tab and select TLS Configuration > Edit inline.
11. Specify the **Key Store Configuration** with the generated keystore details to enable HTTPS on
    this configuration.
12. Click **OK**.

### Configure a Global Element for the Properties File

Configure a global element for the `mule-app.properties` file so that Mule knows where to find it:

1.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
2.  In the **Choose Global Type** dialog, select **Configuration properties** and click **OK**.
3.  In the **File** field, enter `mule.app.properties`.
4.  Click **OK**.

## Get a Job

This Mule flow retrieves a job from the given job ID.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Get Job**  
  Gets a job

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>attributes.queryParams.jobId</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Get Job** operation

![Studio Flow for the Get Job operation](_images/get-job.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="getQueryFlow"
		  >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"

					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/get" />
		<bigquery:get-job doc:name="Get Job"

						  jobId="#[attributes.queryParams.jobId]"
						  config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration" />
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"
				 />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Insert a Job

This Mule flow inserts a job using the input configuration.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Insert Job**  
  Insert a job

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Job values content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>payload</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Insert Job** operation

![Studio Flow for the Insert Job operation](_images/insert-job.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="insertJobFlow"
		  >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"

					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/insert" />
		<bigquery:insert-job doc:name="Insert Job"

							 config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration">
			<bigquery:job-values-content><![CDATA[#[payload]]]></bigquery:job-values-content>
		</bigquery:insert-job>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"
				 />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Delete a Job

This Mule flow deletes a job from the given job ID.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Delete Job**  
  Deletes a job

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>attributes.queryParams.jobId</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Delete Job** operation

![Studio Flow for the Delete Job operation](_images/delete-job.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="deleteJobFlow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/delete" />
		<bigquery:delete-job doc:name="Delete Job"
							 config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration"
							 jobId="#[attributes.queryParams.jobId]" />
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Get a Query Result

This Mule flow retrieves results of a query.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Get Query Results**  
  Get results of a query

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>attributes.queryParams.jobId</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Start Index</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Results</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>25</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Get Query Results** operation

![Studio Flow for the Get Query Results operation](_images/get-query-result.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="getQueryResultFlow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/get/results" />
		<bigquery:get-query-results doc:name="Get Query Result Paged" config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration" maxResults="25" jobId="#[attributes.queryParams.jobId]">
			<ee:repeatable-file-store-iterable inMemoryObjects="1000" />
		</bigquery:get-query-results>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"
			 message="#[payload]" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Cancel a Job

This Mule flow cancels a job from the given job ID.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Cancel Job**  
  Cancels a job

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>attributes.queryParams.jobId</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>application/json</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Cancel Job** operation

![Studio Flow for the Cancel Job operation](_images/cancel-job.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="cancelJobFlow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/cancel" />
		<bigquery:cancel-job doc:name="Cancel Job"
							 config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration"
							 jobId="#[attributes.queryParams.jobId]" />
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## List Jobs

This Mule flow retrieves jobs from the project.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **List Jobs**  
  Lists jobs

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>application/json</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Results</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pretty Print</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>False (Default)</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **List Jobs** operation

![Studio Flow for the List Jobs operation](_images/list-jobs.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="listJobsFlow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/list" />
		<bigquery:list-jobs doc:name="List Jobs"
							config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration" />
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Query

This Mule flow executes the query given in the query job values.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Query**  
  Executes a query

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Query values content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><div><pre><code data-lang="java">%dw 2.0
  output application/json
  ---

  payload</code></pre></div></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **For Each**  
  Iterates through multiple components

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Counter Variable Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>counter</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Batch Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Root Message Variable Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>rootMessage</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
  - **Transform Message**  
    Outputs the results of the **Query** operation in JSON format

    %dw 2.0 output application/json var resultt = read(payload."output","application/json")

    ***

    ((resultt.f) map ((item, index) -> {(payload.attributes.tableSchema.fields\[index\].name as
    String) : item.v} )) reduce ((item, accumulator) -> accumulator ++ item)

  - **Logger**  
    Shows the HTTP response from the **Query** operation

![Studio Flow for the Query operation](_images/query.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<mule xmlns:google-drive="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/google-drive"
	  xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/google-drive http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/google-drive/current/mule-google-drive.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>
	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="queryFlow">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"
					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/query" />
		<bigquery:query doc:name="Run Query create schema" config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration" responseTimeout="300" responseTimeoutUnit="SECONDS" >
			<bigquery:query-values-content ><![CDATA[#[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]]></bigquery:query-values-content>
		</bigquery:query>
		<foreach doc:name="For Each" >
			<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
				<ee:message>
					<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
var resultt = read(payload."output","application/json")
---
((resultt.f) map ((item, index) ->
        {(payload.attributes.tableSchema.fields[index].name as String) : item.v}
    ))
    reduce ((item, accumulator) -> accumulator ++ item)
]]></ee:set-payload>
				</ee:message>
			</ee:transform>
			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" message="#[payload]"/>
		</foreach>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Use On Table Row

This Mule flow uses the **On Table Row** source.

This example uses the following operations:

- **On Table Row**  
  Returns all rows of a table based on a watermark

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>Fixed Frequency</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>1000</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Start delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS (Default)</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Dataset name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>test124</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Table name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>table_data</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Watermark column</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>id</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Column id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>id</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **On Table Row** source
- **Transform Message**  
  Outputs the results of the **On Table Row** source in JSON format

  %dw 2.0 output application/json

  ***

  payload

![Studio Flow for the On Table Row source](_images/on-table-row.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="onTableRow">
		<bigquery:on-table-row doc:name="On Table Row"
							   config-ref="BigQuery__Configuration" datasetName="test124"
							   tableName="table_data" watermarkColumn="id" since="'1'"
							   columnId="id">
			<scheduling-strategy>
				<fixed-frequency />
			</scheduling-strategy>
		</bigquery:on-table-row>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" />
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message">
			<ee:message>
				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Authorize

This Mule flow authorizes the connection.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Request**  
  Consumes an HTTP service

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Method</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>GET (Default)</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>/authorize?resourceOwnerId=demo</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>payload</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Request** operation

![Studio Flow for the Request operation](_images/authorize.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="authorize">
		<http:listener doc:name="/authorize"
					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/authorize">
			<http:response>
				<http:headers><![CDATA[#[{'content-type' : 'text/html'}]]]></http:headers>
			</http:response>
		</http:listener>
		<http:request method="GET" doc:name="Request"
					  config-ref="HTTP_Request_configuration"
					  path="/authorize?resourceOwnerId=demo">
		</http:request>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"
			 message="#[payload]" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## Render an HTML Form

This Mule flow renders the HTML form.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Parse Template**  
  Processes a template

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Location</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>index.html</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

![Studio Flow for the Parse Template component](_images/html-form.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	  xmlns:bigquery="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery"
	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	  xmlns:secure-properties="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties"
	  xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/bigquery/current/mule-bigquery.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
		http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/secure-properties/current/mule-secure-properties.xsd">
	<secure-properties:config name="Secure_Properties_Config_demo" doc:name="Secure Properties Config"  file="mule-artifact.properties" key="mulesoft" >
		<secure-properties:encrypt algorithm="Blowfish" />
	</secure-properties:config>

	<configuration-properties
			file="mule-artifact.properties" />
	<http:request-config
			name="HTTP_Request_configuration"
			doc:name="HTTP Request configuration"
			>
		<http:request-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								 port="8087"/>
	</http:request-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config"
						  doc:name="HTTP Listener config"
						  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0"
								  port="8082" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<http:listener-config
			name="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" doc:name="HTTP Listener config">
		<http:listener-connection host="127.0.0.1"
								  port="8087" usePersistentConnections="false"
								  connectionIdleTimeout="4000" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<bigquery:config name="BigQuery__Configuration"
					 doc:name="BigQuery  Configuration"
					 >
		<bigquery:oauth2-connection projectId="${config.projectId}">
			<bigquery:oauth-authorization-code
					consumerKey="${secure::config.consumerKey}"
					consumerSecret="${secure::config.consumerSecret}"
					resourceOwnerId="demo" />
			<bigquery:oauth-callback-config
					listenerConfig="HTTP_Listener_config1_Test" callbackPath="/callback"
					authorizePath="/authorize" />
		</bigquery:oauth2-connection>
	</bigquery:config>
	<flow name="html-form-flow">
		<http:listener doc:name="/"
					   config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/">
			<http:response>
				<http:headers><![CDATA[#[{'content-type' : 'text/html'}]]]></http:headers>
			</http:response>
		</http:listener>
		<parse-template doc:name="Parse Template"
						location="index.html" />
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Click the **Authorize** button for OAuth2 authorization.
4.  From a web browser, test the application by entering `http://{app-name}.cloudhub.io`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Google BigQuery Connector 1.2 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

---

### Configuration

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Config_Jwt">JWT Connection</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_Oauth2">Oauth2 Connection</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### JWT Connection

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Project Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Project ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Base Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant gets its own base URI.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>https://bigquery.googleapis.com/bigquery/v2</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default headers to include in the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query parameters to include in the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Persistent Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stream Response</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name app</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Parameter app name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>appName1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Private key ID for the Google connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Private key for the Google connected app.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Issuer</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Client email.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Audience Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The audience identifies the authorization server as an intended audience. The authorization server must verify that it is an intended audience for the token. Use the authorization serverâs URL for the audience value: <code>https://bigquery.googleapis.com/</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>https://bigquery.googleapis.com/</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timeout for the token.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>3600</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Token Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of: <strong>HTTP </strong>HTTPS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Protocol to use for communication.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>TLS configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Oauth2 Connection

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Project Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Project ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Base Uri</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant gets its own parameter base URI.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>https://bigquery.googleapis.com/bigquery/v2</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default headers to include in the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Query Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Query parameters to include in the request.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Persistent Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Connections</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Proxy Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Stream Response</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Name app</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Parameter app name.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>appName1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access_type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to refresh access tokens when the user is not present at the browser.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>online</p><div><p>Retrieves an access token.</p></div></li><li><p>offline</p><div><p>Retrieves a refresh token and an access token.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>offline</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prompt</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Prompt.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>consent</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Refresh Token Condition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Refresh condition.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Protocol</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Protocol to use for communication.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorization Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the service providerâs authorization endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Token Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the service providerâs access token endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scopes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/bigquery</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource Owner Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Resource owner ID to use with the authorization code grant type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Before</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>After</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after receiving an access token.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External Callback Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Cancel Job](#CancelJob)
- [Delete Job](#DeleteJob)
- [Get Job](#GetJob)
- [Get Query Results](#GetQueryResults)
- [Insert Job](#InsertJob)
- [List Jobs](#ListJobs)
- [Query](#Query)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)

## Associated Sources

- [On Table Row](#OnTableRow)

## Operations

### Cancel Job

`<bigquery:cancel-job>`

Cancels a job. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/project/{projectId}/jobs/{jobId}/cancel endpoint.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Job ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Content type.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>application/json</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Query Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

#### Throws

- BIGQUERY:BAD_REQUEST
- BIGQUERY:CLIENT_ERROR
- BIGQUERY:CONNECTIVITY
- BIGQUERY:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- BIGQUERY:NOT_FOUND
- BIGQUERY:PROJECT_NOT_FOUND
- BIGQUERY:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- BIGQUERY:SERVER_ERROR
- BIGQUERY:TIMEOUT
- BIGQUERY:UNAUTHORIZED

### Delete Job

`<bigquery:delete-job>`

Deletes a job. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /project/{projectId}/job/{jobId}
endpoint.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Job ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Query Parameters</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Custom Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the request times out.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

#### Throws

- BIGQUERY:BAD_REQUEST
- BIGQUERY:CLIENT_ERROR
- BIGQUERY:CONNECTIVITY
- BIGQUERY:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- BIGQUERY:NOT_FOUND
- BIGQUERY:PROJECT_NOT_FOUND
- BIGQUERY:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- BIGQUERY:SERVER_ERROR
- BIGQUERY:TIMEOUT
- BIGQUERY:UNAUTHORIZED

### Get Job

`<bigquery:get-job>`

Gets a job. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /project/{projectId}/jobs/{jobId}
endpoint.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Job ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Config Ref</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams.
